title: Adding and subtracting
summary: Adding and subtracting in Calculated Columns and SQL Expressions

sketchBlocks.title: Using Arithmetic in SQL SELECT
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
SQL allows you to perform arithmetic operations directly in your SELECT statements. For example, you can add or subtract numeric columns to create new calculated columns in your result set. This is useful for generating new values from existing data, such as adjusting grades or calculating totals.

Example:

```sql
SELECT name, grade_level + 1 AS next_year_grade
FROM students;
```

This query shows each student's name and what their grade level will be next year.

sketchBlocks.title: Subtracting Values in SQL
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
You can also subtract values in SQL expressions. For instance, if you want to see how many years until a student graduates (assuming graduation is at grade 12), you can subtract their current grade from 12.

Example:

```sql
SELECT name, 12 - grade_level AS years_until_graduation
FROM students;
```

This query calculates how many years each student has left until graduation.

quizDraft.title: Add a Value to a Column
quizDraft.prompt: Suppose you want to show each student's grade level next year. Which SQL expression correctly adds 1 to the current grade_level?
quizDraft.template: SELECT name, grade_level ___ 1 AS next_year_grade FROM students;

help.concept: In SQL, arithmetic operators like +, -, *, and / are used to perform calculations on numeric columns.
help.hint_1: To add a value, use the addition operator.
help.hint_2: The plus sign (+) is used for addition in SQL expressions.

quizDraft.title: Subtracting for Graduation Countdown
quizDraft.prompt: To find out how many years a student has until graduation (grade 12), which value should fill the blank?
quizDraft.template: SELECT name, 12 ___ grade_level AS years_left FROM students;
quizDraft.hint: Think about how to calculate the difference.

help.concept: Subtraction in SQL lets you find the difference between two numbers, such as years remaining.
help.hint_1: To find how much is left, subtract the current value from the total.
help.hint_2: The minus sign (-) is used for subtraction in SQL.

quizDraft.title: Understanding SQL Expressions
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following best describes a calculated column in SQL?
options: A column that is copied directly from the table.
A column created by performing an operation on existing columns.
A column that always contains text values.
A column that cannot be renamed.
quizDraft.hint: Calculated columns use expressions to create new values.

help.concept: A calculated column is generated by applying an expression or operation to one or more existing columns in a SELECT statement.
help.hint_1: Look for the option that involves creating new values from existing data.
help.hint_2: Calculated columns are not just copies; they are the result of expressions.

quizDraft.title: Valid Arithmetic Expressions in SQL
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are valid ways to use arithmetic in a SQL SELECT statement? (Choose all that apply.)
options: SELECT name, grade_level FROM students WHERE grade_level / 2 = 5;
quizDraft.hint: All standard arithmetic operators can be used in SQL expressions.

help.concept: SQL supports addition (+), subtraction (-), multiplication (*), and division (/) in SELECT statements and WHERE clauses.
help.hint_1: Check if each example uses a valid arithmetic operator and syntax.
help.hint_2: All four statements use arithmetic correctly in SQL.

quizDraft.title: Write a Query with Addition and Subtraction
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query that shows each student's name, their current grade_level, and what their grade will be two years from now (call it future_grade).
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT name, grade_level, grade_level + 2 AS future_grade FROM students;
quizDraft.hint: You need to select name, grade_level, and a new column that adds 2 to grade_level.

help.concept: You can create new columns in SQL by using arithmetic expressions and assigning them an alias with AS.
help.hint_1: Include name and grade_level in your SELECT, then add a new column using grade_level + 2.
help.hint_2: Use AS to give the new column a clear name like future_grade.
